vectors.note: three legal half-moves played in order (each at/after its slot's boundary — the preceding half-move's timing): the full chain is selected

first plays Ra1-a4 @200; second had queued two premoves for their slot (both before the boundary 200) — an illegal Ke8-e6 @50 and a newer legal Ke8-e7 @60. The illegal one is skipped and the newer legal premove wins the anterior window (latest-legal)

first plays Ra1-a4 @200; second queued two LEGAL premoves for their slot — an earlier Ke8-e7 @50 and a later re-premove Ke8-d8 @60 (both before the boundary 200). The anterior window binds the latest legal premove, so the re-premove wins

first plays Ra1-a4 @200; second published both a legal premove Ke8-e7 @50 (anterior, before the boundary 200) and a legal live move Ke8-d8 @300 (informed, after it). The anterior window is tried first, so the legal premove binds and the live move is ineffective

first plays step 1 @100 and a step-2 premove @110 (before second's reply @200); second answers step 1 @200. At the step-2 slot the boundary is 200, so the @110 premove is anterior and, as the only legal candidate, wins the anterior window

first plays Ra1-a4 @100; second's only candidate is an illegal live move Ke8-e6 @200 (informed, after the boundary 100): it is skipped, the slot is unfilled (no illegalmove), and the chain stops at the first half-move
a third half-move timed after the cutoff is excluded; the chain stops at the second
first plays; the opponent never fills their slot, so the chain stops at one half-move (the slot stays unfilled and the on-move player's clock runs — adjudication resolves it)
